summary.intent: Implement a secure ReadSkillTool for the moltis gateway that lets the AI agent read skill files without leaking absolute paths, then harden it with hermes-agent-inspired robustness improvements (binary file handling, assets/ directory support, sorted sidecar listings).
summary.outcome: Delivered two commits: the initial ReadSkillTool implementation (PR #630, commit 66ad3c22) with injection scanning and traversal guards, followed by a robustness follow-up commit adding binary-file detection, assets/ sidecar support, sorted listings, and 23 additional tests (55 tool tests + 8 gateway integration tests, all green).

repo: Skills live under a sidecar directory structure with references/, templates/, scripts/, and assets/ subdirectories alongside the primary SKILL.md.
The gateway registers tools in a skill-tools block using a shared FsSkillDiscoverer instance in crates/gateway/src/server.rs.
Path safety reuses normalize_relative_skill_file_path from an existing helper — no need to reinvent containment logic.
Pre-existing macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure (tracked as moltis-csq) prevents a full `just lint` run; targeted per-crate clippy is the accepted workaround.
The session-close protocol includes `bd dolt pull` for Dolt sync, but no Dolt remote is configured in ephemeral worktrees.
hermes-agent has a parallel Python skill system (skills_tool.py, skill_view) that was used as a reference for porting robustness patterns.

code.finding: ReadSkillTool implements primary + sidecar read paths with 128 KB cap, symlink-escape rejection, binary-file detection, and assets/ directory support. Containment guards reuse normalize_relative_skill_file_path.
Warn-only injection scanner ported from hermes-agent with 9 patterns and 11 tests — does not block, only annotates.
Dropped path= attribute and absolute SKILL.md path from <available_skills> to prevent path leakage; activation instruction now names read_skill(name=..., file_path=...).
Integration tests cover: registry presence, primary read with sidecar listing, sidecar read, unknown-name error, traversal rejection, assets/ directory, and binary file handling (8 cases total).

workflow: Running targeted per-crate clippy (cargo clippy -p <crate>) is the reliable alternative when full `just lint` is blocked by an unrelated build failure.
Background task notification summaries can be mislabeled (exit code 101 reported as 'completed') — always read the output file to get the true result.
Beads issues were created and immediately closed within the same session for the follow-up robustness work, used as lightweight task tracking.
Format check (cargo fmt --check) was run as a gate before each commit alongside clippy.

friction: Pre-existing macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure (moltis-csq) blocked full `just lint` runs throughout the session, requiring manual per-crate clippy workarounds.
Background task notification for bcalmwoi9 reported exit code 0 in the summary but the actual exit code was 101 — misleading status reporting.
Dolt remote not configured in the ephemeral worktree, causing `bd dolt pull` to fail at session close.
A validate_name call caused a compile error that required an edit fix mid-test iteration.
open_items: moltis-avv: token-budget fallback for ReadSkillTool — intentionally left open per the original plan.
moltis-csq: pre-existing macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure blocking full lint runs — unrelated to this work but needs resolution.
The commit is local-only in the ephemeral worktree; PR #630 was pushed but the worktree branch is ephemeral and may not persist.
